_metadata.created_by: Session G verification retrofit (Claude Opus 4.7, 1M context)
_metadata.honesty_note: R6 brief in this folder was written 2026-04-23 from the plan-coherence-case.md S5 specification (Session E never ran). Most of its content is structural/plausibility argument with few numerical claims. The main numerical claim is the photon-mass-vs-FRB-bound comparison. Wang 2023 bound verified 2026-04-24 via Python + Wolfram. Session E when it runs will enrich this folder; for now, this verification only covers R6's internal claims (NOT the full Session E treatment of L1/L2/L3).

m_photon_GCM_kg_claim.source: GCM claim referenced in research/sources/2026-04-23-adjacent-programs.md and plan-coherence-case.md S5

m_photon_GCM_eV_claim.source: plausibility.md restatement; claimed equivalent of 1e-54 kg

FRB_bound_Wang2023_eV.source: Wang, Ran, Zhang 2023 JCAP (arXiv:2304.14784) — FRB dispersion-measure photon-mass upper bound

orders_below_bound_claim.source: R6/plausibility.md claims GCM value is ~4 orders below Wang bound

DES_time_dilation_exponent_claim.source: DES Supernova Program 2024 (arXiv:2406.05050), measured (1+z)^b with b = 1.003 ± 0.005 (stat) ± 0.010 (sys), 1,504 SNe Ia, z ~ 0.1–1.2

R6_prediction_exponent.source: R6 structural cascade claim: z ∝ λ ∝ d_internal ∝ τ so observed exponent is exactly 1
